evals.expected_output: Multiple logical commits grouping related changes, with conventional commit messages. Tests and implementation should be grouped together. Should present a plan before executing.
expectations: Runs git status and git diff before proposing any commits
Checks git log for existing commit message conventions
Groups test file changes with their corresponding implementation file changes in the same commit
Presents a commit plan to the user before executing any git commit commands
Uses conventional commit prefixes (feat, fix, refactor, test, chore, etc.)
Does not use git add . or git add -A
Uses HEREDOC format for commit messages
Does not run git reset HEAD

evals.expected_output: Analyzes the working tree, identifies distinct concerns, and proposes separate commits for each. Should handle the case where changes in the same file may belong to different commits.
expectations: Reads the actual diff content to understand what changed, not just which files changed
Identifies distinct logical concerns across the changeset
Proposes more than one commit when changes span multiple concerns
Each proposed commit has a clear conventional commit message
Waits for user confirmation before executing commits
Does not auto-stage untracked files without mentioning them

evals.expected_output: Handles a simple commit request by still doing reconnaissance, understanding intent, and writing a good commit message. Even for simple cases, should not blindly add everything.
expectations: Runs reconnaissance commands (git status, git diff) before committing
Does not blindly run git add . even for a simple 'commit this' request
Proposes a commit message using conventional commit format
Handles untracked files appropriately — either includes them with justification or mentions them in a single grouped question, not individually
